description: Mobile web phase A5 (docs/design/native-app-surfaces.md): Chrome on a managed Android emulator. On a capable host (Linux + KVM with a running or bootable emulator) the android context resolves its default device, opens one Chrome session through the lazily-installed UiAutomator2 driver (chromedriver auto-downloaded to match the device's Chrome), and goTo/find/click/screenshot behave exactly as on desktop — element semantics are web DOM. On every incapable host the android preflight lands the context SKIPPED (no hardware acceleration) — asserted gating, not a gap. The page is served by the job's test server on the HOST at port 8092; from the emulator the host loopback is 10.0.2.2, hence the URL.

tests.description: No `browsers` key on the android entry: the platform default (chrome) fills in. Navigate, assert page text, click a button, assert the click's side effect, and capture the device screen.
Explicit `browsers: chrome` on the android entry — the authored form of the same supported pair.
